The Cell Cycle was first discovered and studied with elegant experiments in Biochemistry and Cell Biology

Cell free system

For many years cell biologists watched the puppet show of DNA synthesis, mitosis, and cytokinesis but had no idea of what lay behind the curtain controlling these events.  The cell-cycle control system was simply a black box inside the cell.  It was not even clear whether there was a separate control system, or whether the processes of DNA synthesis, mitosis and cytokinesis somehow controlled themselves.    A major breakthrough came in the late 1980’s with the identification of the key proteins of the control system, along with the realization that they are distinct from the proteins that perform the processes of DNA replication, chromosome segregation, and so on.
-Alberts et al., 2002

Dependent events                  Cell cycle control system
Models for control of cell cycle

The 2001 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
8 October 2001
The Nobel Assembly at Karolinska Institutet has today decided to award The Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for 2001jointly to
Leland H. Hartwell, R. Timothy (Tim) Hunt and Paul M. Nurse
for their discoveries of
"key regulators of the cell cycle"
Tim Hunt found the proteins that came and went as a function of cell cycle, named them “cyclins”

Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Are eukaryotic

How can you identify genes and the corresponding proteins that are important to a specific phenomenon?
Make mutants that affect what you are interested in

Yeast can propagate as haploids.
You can knock out a gene in the haploid.
(In eukaryotic systems that are diploid, you often have to make the mutation homozygous to see the function of the gene.)

Life cycle of the yeast Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Mating types: a and a

How do you make mutants of processes that are essential to life?
Isolate CONDITIONAL mutants!

Temperature Sensitive Mutations
Allows you to have mutations in genes that would normally give a lethal phenotype
(Replica plate)
Conditional mutant that blocks cell-cycle at G1/S transition

Cell Division Cycle Mutants
L. Hartwell isolated 148 CDC mutants

If you have a zillion cell cycle mutants, how can you tell how many are in different genes?
Complementation Assays
(148 mutants, 32 genes Hartwell et al. 1973).

How can you use genetics to tell which protein is working first?
Make double mutants

How do you make a double mutant?
Independent Assortment if they are not linked
Recombination if the genes are linked

Isolation of cloned DNA corresponding to yeast mutants (Mutant Rescue)
Isolate DNA from wild-type yeast
Cut yeast DNA and vector with restriction enzymes
Ligate yeast fragments into vector
(1 wild-type gene/vector)
Transform into mutant yeast and select for 				transformants
Select for rescue of mutant gene by wild-type DNA
Isolate and characterize plasmid that rescues.

In Schizosaccharomyces pombe
fission yeast

There are multiple cyclins

G1 cyclins in Saccharomyces cerevisiae were isolated as suppressors of cdc28 mutations
Wild type yeast genomic DNA cut and ligated into high copy number vector

CLN1 and CLN2 were identified as G1 cyclins (later found CLN3)
